column1: I also buy spell components if you are interested in gathering some for me. Don't mind me, I always have so much to do around here.
column2: 「我也收購法術材料，有興趣幫我採集的話儘管說。別理我，我這兒事情多得很。」

column1: You see a clean but very cluttered little hut filled with bottles and potions.  The Healer is busy puttering around with her potions.
column2: 你看見一間乾淨但凌亂不堪的小屋，擺滿了瓶瓶罐罐和各式藥水。治療師正忙著在她的藥水堆裡翻騰。

column1: There is a small flying creature preening itself on the rafter.  A ladder leads to the loft above.  A pot over the fire simmers with herbs.  It is pleasantly warm in here.
column2: 一隻小飛行生物在梁上理羽毛。一把梯子通向上面的閣樓。火上的鍋子裡飄著草藥香。這兒溫暖舒服極了。

column1: You can buy potions, get information, or sell ingredients here.
column2: 你可以在這裡買藥水、打聽消息，或賣材料。

column1: There is an aura of magic throughout this small cabin.
column2: 整間小屋籠罩著魔法氣息。

column1: Come back in the morning, please.
column2: 「請早上再來。」

column1: You don't really think that is the correct way to win this game, do you?
column2: 你不會真的認為那是贏遊戲的正確方法吧？

column1: You see bottles and flasks of a wide variety of different potions.
column2: 你看見各式各樣的瓶子和燒瓶裡裝著五花八門的藥水。

column1: You carefully grab a couple of Healing Potions and conceal them under your cape.
column2: 你小心翼翼地抓起幾瓶治療藥水，藏在你的披風下。

column1: If you're going to steal, at least wait until the Healer is not looking.
column2: 你要偷的話，至少等治療師不看的時候。

column1: You're getting greedy.  She'll notice if you take any more.
column2: 你貪心了。你再拿她就會發現的。

column1: The healer uses this table as a workbench.
column2: 治療師把這張桌子當成工作台。

column1: You see a few odds and ends (not to mention This and That).
column2: 你看見各式各樣的東西（更別提那些雜七雜八的）。

column1: The view from the window has been obscured by soot from the fire.
column2: 窗戶外的景色被爐火的煙灰遮住了。

column1: It's snow.
column2: 那是雪。

column1: The cave looks very eerie and ominous.
column2: 洞穴看起來陰森詭異。

column1: Better watch out -- those icicles look sharp!
column2: 小心點──那些冰錐看起來很尖銳！

column1: Although the cave mouth looks like a mouth, and opens and shuts like a mouth, I'm afraid it doesn't know how to talk like a mouth.
column2: 雖然洞口看起來像嘴巴，開閉起來也像嘴巴，但我怕它不知道怎麼像嘴巴那樣說話。

column1: From the blue color of his skin and his huge height, you can tell this is a Frost Giant.
column2: 從他皮膚的藍色和巨大的身軀，你能看出這是個暴風巨人。

column1: The gem glows like an aurora in the night sky.
column2: 那顆寶石像夜空的極光一樣閃耀。

column1: The Giant looks at you in disgust.  Apparently, you did not offer what he desires.
column2: 巨人厭惡地看著你。看來你沒給他想要的東西。

column1: Bargain
column2: 討價還價

column1: Brauggi
column2: 布勞基

column1: Hunger
column2: 飢餓

column1: Mead
column2: 蜂蜜酒

column1: Northlands
column2: 北國

column1: Find me some fruit for to mellow my mead horn, Gift I will give of a gem that now glows --
column2: 「給我找點水果來緩和我的蜂蜜酒啊，我會賞你這顆會發光的寶石──」

column1: Jewel from Jotunheim, flare of the frost flame, Fetch to me fruit that will fill up my fists!
column2: 「約頓海姆的寶石啊，冰焰的光芒，給我拿來滿滿一拳頭的水果！」

column1: (It looks like it will take a lot of fruit to fill up THOSE fists!)
column2: （要填滿那樣的拳頭看來得花不少水果啊！）

column1: Brauggi I be, to boast of my boldness, Strong as the storm that sends forth the snow.
column2: 「我就是布勞基，以膽識自豪，強似送來白雪的風暴。」

column1: Fiercer in fighting than foes in their fury, Fear now this Frost Giant fighter and flee!
column2: 「戰鬥中比敵人猛，所向披靡，怕我這暴風巨人戰士吧，逃吧！」

column1: Fiercer than frost's bite, my fury is fearless, My axe it is edged as is the ice.
column2: 「比寒冰的咬傷更兇悍，我的怒火無畏，我的斧刃如同冰鎚。」

column1: This warrior is willing to wield now the war drum, The battleaxe blazes and bellows for blood.
column2: 「這戰士如今願意揮舞戰鼓，戰斧熊熊燃燒，呼號著要血！」

column1: Highpoint of harvest, the finest of flavor, Apples or oranges, peaches or pears.
column2: 「豐收的高峰，最上乘的滋味，蘋果或柳橙，桃子或梨子。」

column1: Glow of the frost flame that fills up the night field, A jewel that is flawless, the finest of gems.
column2: 「充滿夜空的冰焰光芒，無瑕的寶石，最精美的珍珠。」

column1: Hollow my food house, for my hunt has been hindered, By having to hurry here from my home.
column2: 「我的食物倉空了啊，因為狩獵受阻，得匆匆從家趕來這裡。」

column1: Mead is the mother's milk, mighty yet mellow That brings joy to Giants and madness to men.
column2: 「蜂蜜酒是母親的奶啊，強大卻溫和，給巨人帶來歡樂，給人類帶來瘋狂。」

column1: Far from the frost field, fares forth this fighter, Hunger has hurled me hither from home.
column2: 「遠離冰雪地啊，這戰士踏上征途，飢荒把我從家鄉硬生生扔到這裡。」

column1: My name it is known in the Northlands as Brauggi, Barter with blade's clash, or bargain with me.
column2: 「北國都知道我叫布勞基，用刀劍碰撞來交易，或者跟我討價還價。」

column1: The log is covered with a layer of late winter snow.
column2: 原木被厚厚的晚冬積雪覆蓋著。

column1: Leave it alone; you don't know where that log's been.
column2: 別碰它；你不知道那根木頭哪來的。

column1: I'm sure that was fun; why don't you talk to a twig next?
column2: 我敢說那很有趣；下一個何不去跟樹枝說話？

column1: You can see a small patch of sky through the trees.
column2: 你能透過樹木看到一小片天空。

column1: That's it; reach for the sky!
column2: 就是那樣；夠著天空吧！

column1: It's a bit late in the season for skiing.
column2: 這季節滑雪有點晚了。

column1: Maybe if you reach those mountains, you'll become a true hero.
column2: 或許如果你登上那些山峰，你就會成為真正的英雄。

column1: I don't think talking to a mountain is healthy.
column2: 我不覺得跟山說話是健康的舉動。

column1: Fruit you have found, to fill all my food stores, Thus filled the bargain, my gem you have bought.
column2: 「果實你找到了，填飽了我的食物倉，交易這樣成了，寶石就是你的。」

column1: Brauggi has bartered, and all has been answered; The mead it may mellow, and now I head home.
column2: 「布勞基完成交易了，一切都成了；蜂蜜酒能讓我舒坦，如今我要回家了。」

column1: Courage you carry,  but skill it is scanty; Face not this foe  before fiercer you fight.
column2: 「你有膽量啊，但技巧還差得遠；別跟我這敵手交手，除非你變得更兇悍。」

column1: Brauggi strides off through the cave on his way back to his Northern home.
column2: 布勞基大步踏過洞穴，踏上回北國家園的征途。

column1: Your fruit definitely makes a generous pile in the Giant's cupped hands, but they're not full yet.
column2: 你的水果在巨人的雙手窩裡堆成不錯的一堆，但還沒滿。

column1: Running on the ice is apparently not a very good idea.
column2: 在冰上跑看起來不是什麼好主意。

column1: As you begin to cast your spell...
column2: 當你開始施法時…

column1: Oops.  You're not carrying any fruit for the Frost Giant.
column2: 哎呀。你身上沒有暴風巨人要的水果。

column1: Your meagre selection of fruit makes a paltry pile at the bottom of the Giant's cupped hands.  You'll need to get quite a few more apples to fill THOSE hands!
column2: 你那可憐兮兮的水果在巨人雙手窩底堆成一小堆。你得多拿好幾個蘋果才能填滿那樣的手！

column1: As you start to throw...
column2: 當你開始投擲時…

column1: The mountains rise quickly from this point and are covered with snow.  To the west, you can see that the snow has melted in most of the forest, but here it is still heavy.
column2: 山峰從此處陡峭上升並覆滿白雪。往西看，你能看到森林大部分的雪已融化，但這邊的雪還厚著呢。

column1: Brauggi has departed on the long journey to his homeland.
column2: 布勞基已踏上返回家鄉的漫長征途。

column1: Brauggi has become impatient with your questions and gone elsewhere.  You will have to return another time.
column2: 布勞基對你的問題不耐煩了，去別的地方了。你得改天再來。

column1: The Giant (who does not seem pleased at your casting a spell in his presence) radiates a magical aura.  The strongest magic centers on the glowing gem at his belt.
column2: 巨人（看起來對你在他面前施法不太高興）散發著魔法氣息。最強的魔法聚集在他腰上那顆會發光的寶石上。

column1: The blue in the sky is just like the blue sky of your home.  You feel a sudden pang of homesickness, and begin to understand Brauggi's loneliness.
column2: 天空的藍色就像你家鄉的藍天。你突然湧起鄉愁，開始理解布勞基的孤寂。

column1: To touch the sky, what dreams you have!
column2: 想觸摸天空，你的夢想好遠啊！

column1: Snow covers rock, scissors cut snow, and rock breaks scissors.  It's very Zen.
column2: 雪蓋住石頭，剪刀剪碎雪，石頭砸爛剪刀。很禪嘛。

column1: Before you take that snow and put it in your mouth, ask yourself a question: "Do I feel lucky?"
column2: 在你把那雪拿進嘴裡之前，問問自己一個問題：「我感覺幸運嗎？」

column1: The rock seems to listen patiently, but doesn't respond.
column2: 石頭好像在耐心傾聽，但沒有回應。

column1: Snow covers these rocks like a blanket.
column2: 雪像毯子一樣覆蓋著這些石頭。

column1: It would be fun to make a snowball, but you really haven't time.
column2: 堆雪球是很有趣，但你真的沒時間。

column1: There's snow all around this area.  In fact, it seems rather colder here than in the rest of the valley.
column2: 這地方四周都是雪。事實上，這兒看起來比山谷其他地方冷得多。

column1: It would be fun to make a snowball, but you really haven't time.
column2: 堆雪球是很有趣，但你真的沒時間。

column1: It's a small patch of yellow snow.
column2: 那是一小片黃雪。

column1: The last thing you need is a yellow snowball.
column2: 你最不需要的就是黃雪球。

column1: You look at the apple cores, and quickly decide they are of no use to you. Besides, they look disgusting.
column2: 你看著蘋果核，迅速決定它們對你沒用。而且它們看起來噁心得要死。

column1: The baby Meep is just too quick for you.  Besides, they eat too much.
column2: 小咪普快得你根本追不上。而且它們食量太大了。

column1: The baby Meep sure is cute.
column2: 小咪普真的很可愛。

column1: You keep seeing differently-colored Meeps come out of each hole.  They either lead to large underground caverns, or they are all connected under the earth.
column2: 你不斷看見不同顏色的咪普從每個洞冒出來。它們要嘛通向大型地下洞穴，要嘛全部在地底連通。

column1: There is a wad of bright green fur on the ground.
column2: 地上有一團亮綠色的毛皮。

column1: The meadow is covered with a light carpet of grass, broken up by the Meep holes.
column2: 草地被薄薄的草皮覆蓋著，中間散佈著咪普的洞。

column1: Hey, like, we eat them, you know.
column2: 「嘿，咦，我們吃它們啊，你知道吧。」

column1: Cute, huh?  The tunnels down here just crawl with those things.  Get in the way all the time, you know?
column2: 「可愛吧？底下的隧道裡到處都是那些傢伙。老是擋路，你知道吧？」

column1: Gee boss, I really don't know much about that at all.  Hey, sorry.
column2: 「天啊老大，我對那個真的不太了解。嘿，抱歉。」

column1: Gee boss, I really don't know much about that at all.  Hey, sorry.
column2: 「天啊老大，我對那個真的不太了解。嘿，抱歉。」

column1: Hey, like fur is good stuff, keeps us warm.  Mine's the best -- it's like green, you know?
column2: 「嘿，咦，毛皮是好東西啊，能讓我們暖和。我的最讚──是綠色的，你知道吧？」

column1: Oh, you want some green fur?  I think I have some somewhere.
column2: 「哦，你想要綠毛皮？我想我某個地方放著呢。」

column1: We, like, live in tunnels under the ground, you know.
column2: 「咦，我們住在地底的隧道裡，你知道吧。」

column1: I found a scroll lying around in the woods one day.  You want it?  It's yours.
column2: 「我有天在樹林裡找到一卷捲軸。你想要？它就是你的。」

column1: We are happy Meeps, living in our happy holes.  Don't worry.  Be happy!
column2: 「我們是快樂的咪普，住在我們快樂的洞裡。別擔心。要開心！」

column1: We use rocks for doors.  They keep us dry and warm in our holes.
column2: 「我們用石頭當門。它們讓我們的洞裡乾爽又暖和。」

column1: That thing?  I found it lying around in the woods one day.  You want it? It's yours.
column2: 「那東西？我有天在樹林裡找到的。你想要嗎？它就是你的。」

column1: The Meep scrambles back into its hole as you try to touch it.
column2: 當你試著碰咪普時，它匆匆鑽回洞裡。

column1: You realize that you have never seen more than one green Meep at a time. Perhaps there is only one.
column2: 你意識到你從沒同時見過一隻以上的綠咪普。或許只有一隻。

column1: There seems to be an abundant supply of blue and purple Meeps.  If only they'd stay still for a moment, you'd be able to count them.
column2: 看起來有大量的藍咪普和紫咪普。要是它們肯靜一下，你就能數清楚了。

column1: The Meeps have brightly-colored, soft-looking fur.
column2: 咪普長著色彩繽紛、軟呼呼的毛皮。

column1: Apples
column2: 蘋果

column1: Baby
column2: 寶寶

column1: Hey, just ask me about anything you want to know.
column2: 「嘿，有什麼你想知道的儘管問我。」

column1: Fur
column2: 毛皮

column1: Holes
column2: 洞穴

column1: Meeps
column2: 咪普

column1: Rocks
column2: 石頭

column1: Scroll
column2: 捲軸

column1: You hear squeaky muttering from beneath the ground.  It seems the Meeps are having quite a discussion about you.
column2: 你聽見地底傳來吱吱嘎嘎的碎碎念。看來咪普們正在熱烈討論你呢。

column1: You don't need to do that.
column2: 你不需要那樣做。

column1: You walk away from the hole to get out of the rock's way.
column2: 你走開了，讓開那塊石頭。

column1: You pick up the green fur.
column2: 你撿起綠毛皮。

column1: The magical runes fade from the paper before your eyes can focus on them.
column2: 魔法符文在你的眼睛聚焦之前就從紙上褪去了。

column1: You pick up the Spell Scroll.  As the magical runes fade, you find you now know how to cast the "Detect Magic" spell.
column2: 你撿起魔法捲軸。當符文褪去時，你發現自己現在知道怎麼施展「偵測魔法」法術了。

column1: Hiya, hiya. Nice to seeya 'gain.
column2: 「嘿啊，嘿啊。很高興又見到你。」

column1: Hiya, hiya.  Pleased to meetcha.
column2: 「嘿啊，嘿啊。很高興認識你。」

column1: Hearing a rumble from the Meep hole, you quickly slip out of the way.
column2: 聽見咪普洞裡傳來隆隆聲，你迅速閃到一邊。

column1: You detect no other magic here.
column2: 你在這裡沒有偵測到其他魔法。

column1: All of the Meeps are hiding deep within their holes.  Your vicious attack has them terrified.
column2: 所有咪普都躲在洞裡深處。你的凶悍攻擊嚇得它們要死。

column1: You see blue Meeps, purple Meeps, and occasionally a green Meep.  They seem to be very shy; whenever you approach one, it hides under its rock.
column2: 你看見藍咪普、紫咪普，偶爾還有綠咪普。它們看起來超害羞；你一靠近，它就躲到石頭底下。

column1: You are in the Meeps' Peep.  The colorful, furry Meeps timidly pop out of their holes from time to time.
column2: 你在咪普的窩裡。色彩繽紛、毛茸茸的咪普們不時膽小地冒出洞口。

column1: The Open spell only works on locks and small closed things.
column2: 「開啟術」只對鎖和小型密閉物品有效。

column1: You can't talk to any of the Meeps. They are all hiding deep within their holes, terrified after your vicious attack.
column2: 你沒辦法跟任何咪普說話。它們都躲在洞裡深處，被你的凶悍攻擊嚇得要死。

column1: The mountains are very steep here.
column2: 這邊的山峰陡得要命。

column1: Large, heavy-looking rocks cover the many holes.  The Meeps must be stronger than they look to lift these rocks so easily.
column2: 大塊沉重的石頭蓋著許多洞。咪普們要夠強悍才能這麼輕鬆搬起這些石頭。

column1: No way!  Those Meeps are too fast and the rocks too hard for you to want to try that again!
column2: 沒門！那些咪普太快、石頭太硬了，你不會想再試一次！

column1: It looks like a Spell Scroll.
column2: 看起來像一卷魔法捲軸。

column1: The trees and bushes look much like those you've seen elsewhere in the valley.
column2: 樹木和灌木看起來跟你在山谷其他地方見過的很像。

column1: I just flew in from the coast and BOY! are my arms tired.
column2: 「我剛從海邊飛過來，哇，我的手臂累死了。」

column1: Let us spin, then, you and I, when the evening is buried beneath the sky. It must be Tease Eliot, the ghost poet!
column2: 「那麼讓我們旋轉吧，你和我，當傍晚埋沒在天空底下時。」准是逗趣·埃利奧特，那個幽靈詩人！

column1: The lady is lovely as she lofts along, but beware her fatal touch!
column2: 那位女士飄然而過時美麗動人，但當心她致命的觸碰！

column1: The large stone you are facing has been engraved with this message:
column2: 你面前的這塊大石頭上刻著這樣的訊息：

column1: Here lies Lester Moore Gored six times by a wild boar.
column2: 「此墓：萊斯特·摩爾，被野豬戳傷六次。」

column1: No Les, no Moore.
column2: 「沒有萊斯，沒有摩爾。」

column1: The grate is clearly rusted shut.  You won't be able to open it.
column2: 那鐵柵欄明顯生鏽卡死了。你沒辦法打開。

column1: A massive grate covers a hole, keeping you from seeing the bottom.
column2: 一扇巨大的鐵柵欄蓋著一個洞，擋住你的視線不讓你看底下。

column1: The large tombstone seems to have a barely legible epitaph:
column2: 那塊大墓碑上的墓誌銘看起來幾乎無法辨認：
